We should remember our sins against our Creator, repent, and seek forgiveness. We should remember our duties, and begin them, looking to him for grace and strength. This should be done early, while the body is strong, and the spirits are active. When a man has the pain of reviewing a misspent life, his not having given up sin and worldly pleasures until he is forced to say, I have no pleasure in them, makes his sincerity very questionable. Then follows a figurative description of old age and its weaknesses, which presents some difficulties; but the meaning is clear: to show how uncomfortable old age generally is. As verses 2-5 are a figurative description of the weaknesses that usually accompany old age, it notices the circumstances which take place in the hour of death. If sin had not entered into the world, these weaknesses would not have been known. Surely then the aged should reflect on the evil of sin.

Solomon repeats his text: VANITY OF VANITIES, ALL IS VANITY. These are the words of one who could speak from dearly-bought experience of the vanity of the world, which can do nothing to ease men of the burden of sin. As he considered the worth of souls, he gave good heed to what he spoke and wrote; words of truth will always be welcome words. The truths of God are as goads to those who are dull and draw back, and nails to those who are wandering and draw aside; they serve to establish the heart, that we may never neglect our duty, nor be taken from it. The Shepherd of Israel is the Giver of inspired wisdom. Teachers and guides all receive their communications from him. This title is applied in Scripture to the Lord Jesus Christ, the Son of God. The prophets sought diligently to understand what the Spirit of Christ signified when it foretold the sufferings of Christ and the glory that would follow. To write many books was not suited to the shortness of human life, and would be weariness to the writer and to the reader; and it was much more so then than it is now. All things would be vanity and vexation, except they led to this conclusion: That to fear God and keep his commandments is the whole of man. The fear of God includes all the affections of the soul towards him, which are produced by the Holy Spirit. There may be terror where there is no love, or even hatred. But that is different from the gracious fear of God, like the feelings of an affectionate child. The fear of God is often used to represent the whole of true religion in the heart, and includes its practical results in life. Let us attend to the one thing needful, and now come to him as a merciful Savior, who will soon come as an almighty Judge, when he will bring to light the things of darkness and manifest the intentions of all hearts. Why does God record in his word that ALL IS VANITY, but to keep us from deceiving ourselves to our ruin? He makes our duty our advantage. May it be engraved on all our hearts: Fear God, and keep his commandments, for this is all that concerns man.
